title: acetosa pratensis subsp. amplexicaulis
intro: Acetosa pratensis subsp. amplexicaulis, also known as creeping red sorrel, is a subspecies of plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It is a perennial herb characterized by its reddish leaves and stems, and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Acetosa pratensis subsp. amplexicaulis include its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including scurvy, arthritis, and digestive issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to act as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial agent, and it can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves.
